config.hotelName: Montcalm Piccadilly Townhouse, London West End

days.title: May 7 — Arrival · Royal & River
days.note: Arriving noon · hotel check-in · afternoon start

stops.name: Buckingham Palace & Victoria Memorial
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk through Green Park from hotel.
stops.history: Buckingham Palace has been the official London residence of the monarch since 1837. The Victoria Memorial in front, completed in 1924, is crowned by a gilded figure of Victory with a seated Queen Victoria facing The Mall.
stops.todo: Walk along The Mall toward Trafalgar Square for one of London's great vistas.

stops.name: Fortnum & Mason — Diamond Jubilee Tea Salon
stops.history: Fortnum & Mason has stood on Piccadilly since 1707. The Diamond Jubilee Tea Salon on the fourth floor was created to mark the Queen's Diamond Jubilee in 2012 — one of London's most celebrated afternoon tea destinations, with signature turquoise china and three-tier stands of finger sandwiches, scones and pastries.
stops.todo: 🫖Try the Fortnum's blend tea and the coronation chicken finger sandwiches. The views over Piccadilly are beautiful.

stops.name: Fortnum & Mason — Browse the Food Halls
stops.history: The ground floor food halls are legendary — hampers, jams, chocolates, and the famous Fortnum's confectionery counter with hand-made marzipan fruits.
stops.todo: Browse the confectionery counter and pick up a tin of Fortnum's tea.

stops.name: Hatchards Bookshop
stops.history: Hatchards was founded in 1797 and is London's oldest bookshop, a royal warrant holder since 1801. Its distinctive dark green Victorian facade is unchanged in over two centuries.
stops.todo: Passed by on Piccadilly.

stops.name: Duck & Waffle — Rooftop Dinner
stops.travel: 🚇 Tube to Liverpool Street (5 stops from Green Park). The Heron Tower is right at the station exit.
stops.history: Duck & Waffle is London's highest restaurant at 40 floors up, open 24 hours. The signature dish — crispy duck leg confit on a waffle with duck egg and mustard maple syrup — is extraordinary. The panoramic views over the City of London and beyond are unmatched.
stops.todo: 🦆Order: the Duck & Waffle (the signature dish) and the ox cheek doughnut. Watch the City lights come on during dinner. The lift up is an experience in itself.

stops.name: West End Theater Pub
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk from the West End toward Covent Garden.
stops.history: A classic London theater pub with walls covered in original musical theater posters including Oklahoma!, and a lively atmosphere after shows.
stops.todo: The perfect end to an evening in the West End.

stops.name: BBC Broadcasting House
stops.travel: 🚶 Passed by on walk through Marylebone.
stops.history: BBC Broadcasting House has been the home of the BBC since 1932. The striking circular addition glows blue at night, making it one of London's most photogenic buildings after dark.
stops.todo: A beautiful sight at night with the blue-lit circular extension.

days.title: May 8 — Museums & Sky
days.note: Grand breakfast · Westminster · V&A · Sky Garden

stops.name: The Wolseley — Breakfast
stops.history: The Wolseley is one of London's grandest all-day cafés, housed in a 1920s palace built for the Wolseley Motor Company. The soaring arched ceiling, marble floors, and gilded ironwork make breakfast here one of London's great experiences.
stops.todo: 🥐Order the Full Breakfast or Eggs Benedict with smoked salmon. Sit in the main room to absorb the full grandeur.

stops.name: Westminster Abbey
stops.history: Westminster Abbey has been the coronation church since 1066. Nearly every monarch from William the Conqueror to Charles III was crowned here. The intricate Gothic portal with carved figures is one of the finest in England.
stops.todo: 👑Find Poets' Corner and the Coronation Chair. The carved portal on entry is extraordinary.

stops.name: V&A Museum — Cast Courts & Gardens
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk from Westminster through South Kensington.
stops.history: The V&A Cast Courts house one of the world's great collections of plaster casts — including a full-scale cast of Trajan's Column split across two rooms, and Michelangelo's David. The John Madejski Garden is one of London's hidden peaceful courtyards.
stops.todo: 🏛️Find Trajan's Column cast in Room 46a & 46b. The garden café is perfect for a break.

stops.name: The V&A Café — Gamble Room
stops.address: Victoria and Albert Museum, London SW7 2RL
stops.history: The Gamble Room is one of three original refreshment rooms designed for the V&A in 1868. Its extraordinary domed ceiling, Minton ceramic columns, stained glass windows and ornate plasterwork make it arguably the most beautiful café in London.
stops.todo: ☕Look up at the painted ceramic ceiling — it's stunning. Perfect for coffee and cake after the museum.

stops.name: Sky Garden — Sunset Cocktails
stops.travel: 🚇 Tube to Monument. The Walkie-Talkie building is a 3-min walk.
stops.history: Sky Garden occupies the top three floors of 20 Fenchurch Street (the 'Walkie-Talkie'). Free to enter with advance booking, it offers 360° panoramic views of London with a lush garden and bar. The Francis Golding Terrace has spectacular open-air views.
stops.todo: 🌿Book free tickets at skygarden.london. Head to the outdoor terrace for the best city views at sunset. The bar serves excellent cocktails.

days.title: May 9 — Soho & Covent Garden
days.note: Sketch breakfast · Chinatown · Rules dinner · Caffè Concerto

stops.name: Sketch — Breakfast in The Glade
stops.history: Sketch is one of London's most extraordinary restaurants. The Glade is an enchanting woodland-themed room with floor-to-ceiling forest murals, trees hanging from the ceiling, and dappled light — like breakfast in a fairy-tale woodland.
stops.todo: 🌿Order the pastry basket and fresh juices. Sit in one of the alcove booths surrounded by the forest mural. Extraordinary room.

stops.name: Leicester Square — Swiss Glockenspiel
stops.history: The Swiss Glockenspiel clock on Leicester Square was installed in 1985 as a gift from Switzerland, featuring cantonal flags and bell-ringing figurines. M&M's World and the major cinemas surround the square.
stops.todo: Try to be there when the bells chime on the hour.

stops.name: Chinatown — Gerrard Street
stops.history: London's Chinatown has been centred on Gerrard Street since the 1970s. Hundreds of red lanterns hang the length of the street, with restaurants, bakeries, and the ceremonial gate at Wardour Street — a gift from the Chinese government.
stops.todo: Walk the full length under the red lanterns. Try a BBQ pork bun from one of the bakeries.

stops.name: Covent Garden Market
stops.history: Covent Garden Market has traded on this site since the 1650s. The current market building opened in 1830. The piazza is London's most famous street performance venue.
stops.todo: Watch the street performers and browse the Apple Market crafts stalls.

stops.name: Rules Restaurant
stops.history: Rules is London's oldest restaurant, established 1798 by Thomas Rule. For 225+ years it has served traditional British food in its distinctive dining rooms hung with antlers, cartoons and theatrical portraits. Dickens, Galsworthy and Edward VII all dined here.
stops.todo: 🦌Order the game dishes — venison or pheasant in season. The steak and kidney pudding is legendary. Study the extraordinary portraits on the walls.

stops.name: Caffè Concerto
stops.history: Caffè Concerto is an elegant London café chain known for its ornate crystal chandeliers, fresh pink roses on every table, and beautifully presented cakes and light meals. A beautiful, romantic setting for dinner.
stops.todo: 🌹Try the berry cakes and enjoy the elegant atmosphere. The crystal chandeliers are spectacular.

days.title: May 10 — Final Day & Thames Farewell
days.note: Borough Market · The Shard · Thames Dinner Cruise

stops.name: Borough Market — Breakfast
stops.travel: 🚇 Tube to London Bridge (2 stops from Green Park via Jubilee).
stops.history: Borough Market has traded on this site for over 1,000 years, making it London's oldest food market. Today it hosts over 100 stalls of artisan food from around the world.
stops.todo: 🥐Try the Monmouth Coffee and a pastry from Bread Ahead. Don't miss the Kappacasein cheese toastie stall.

stops.name: The Shard — Viewing Gallery
stops.history: The Shard is the tallest building in the UK at 310m. Designed by Renzo Piano, its viewing platform at 244m offers views up to 40 miles on a clear day.
stops.todo: 🏙️Buy tickets in advance online. The best views are from the open-air platform on Level 72. Look for Tower Bridge directly below.

stops.name: Leadenhall Market
stops.travel: 🚇 Tube to Bank from London Bridge (1 stop), then 5-min walk.
stops.history: Leadenhall Market is a covered market in the City of London dating to the 14th century. The stunning Victorian ironwork and glass roof (painted in cream, maroon and green) made it the filming location for Diagon Alley in the Harry Potter films.
stops.todo: 🦁Look up at the ornate Victorian ceiling. Find the optician's shop that became Ollivanders in Harry Potter.

stops.name: Dinner Cruise on the River Thames
stops.address: Thames River, London — check GetYourGuide app for boarding point
stops.travel: 🚢 Check GetYourGuide app for exact boarding location and time.
stops.history: A candlelit dinner cruise along the River Thames — one of London's most romantic experiences. Glide past illuminated landmarks: Houses of Parliament, Tower Bridge, St Paul's, and the Tate Modern as the city lights reflect on the water. The Thames has been London's lifeblood for 2,000 years.
stops.todo:
🛳️Booked — $283 for 2 adults, 3 hours.
Tickets in GetYourGuide app. Arrive 15 min early at the boarding point. Dress smartly. Watch for Tower Bridge lit up at night as you glide underneath.
stops.prebookText: Booked via GetYourGuide — mobile tickets ready
